narrative: Hawkish Fed bets and Jerome Powell’s exit amid surging inflation fears dominate the weekend tape, setting up a USD-bullish open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1631; technical breakdowns and a +163bp OIS carry advantage for the USD likely to maintain downside pressure at the Tokyo open. USD/JPY closed Friday at 158.73; despite a strong -1.73 risk reversal put skew, higher US 10Y yields (4.595%) would support the pair at Sunday's open. Focus shifts to the RBNZ meeting on May 27 as the next key policy catalyst.
